review_text: Nice spot right on the beach and close to town, shops, restaurants etc. Very quiet time of year. Super modern and clean amenities block and camp kitchens. 5 star amenities.

The best accommodation for us every year for our Bowls Carnival.
Lovely clean cabins,friendly staff and what a view and location.

Lovely clean park with good amenities in the cabins and camp kitchen/toilets. Good value for being a few minutes walk to the beach. Walkable into town for good restaurants and shops, although a big grocery store is further away. It was quiet when we were there despite being school holidays. Would return again

New staff and management are amazing, I think the best we have ever had in coming here 3 times a year for 33 years. Sherri is so lovely, even popping around on a regular basis to check in with us. Honestly yes the park needs a little work, but I see how things are changing and how much the staff are doing to make this happen.
Best park ever. Plenty of shade. We had ocean view, which probably helped. And only a short walk to beach. Not patrolled though. Good amenities and plenty of camp kitchens.

impeccably clean, facilities well priced and everything for a unit included, staff friendly and attentive and the grounds well maintained.  you couldn't ask for more from a caravan, camping and cabin park (and i almost never write reviews). thankyou
So close to the beach with a great local cafe at the beach

A nice park though some facilities quite dated. Was nice and close to the beach but the park had too many codes to remember, One for  entry and exit via boom gate, one for entry and exit via pedestrians access , and one for the ablution blocks, if I forgot my phone I would be locked out. Also the park was at capacity and hence the showers were  often lukewarm.
Wifi needs fixing no excuse for shit Wifi these days

Booked a site in April 2025 only to be told we had been moved to a different site when we arrived. No other sites available. Site we were given was too small for our van, was extremely difficult to access and had a terrible slope. Changing our site without consultation is unacceptable.

Booked over phone told them we’d be late checkin. No codes left. No site given. Couldn’t access at 7.00pm. Had our 1 year old with us. Had to wait until management turned up and allocated site and then by darkness was left putting up tent in howling wind with screaming 1 year old. Missed out on dinner at the restaurant.||Won’t be back. Do better.
Staying on a site with caravan.  Nice and tidy, amenities very clean . Close to local shops and not to far from the water

Stayed in a cabin and not impressed with the very late check in time of 3pm. Told us they would call us to try and get us in early. After no phone call we arrived at service desk at 3pm only for cabin to still not be ready. Then had to wait whilst a big line of people also checking in at this time jumped in front of us. After getting into our cabin late we were then immediately refused a request for late checkout saying cleaners needed access to clean our cabin at 10am. So on the day we arrived cleaners didn’t start cleaning to after 2pm but on the day we leave they had to clean at 10am? Very poor service. Facilities are run down too with temporary amenities still sitting in the middle of the park but haven’t been in use for years. Other amenities up near beach also still sitting in park locked up but not in use. Location near beach is fabulous but caravan park is run down and peak season is very expensive given the lack of facilities compared to other parks in the area (ie no playground, no pool, etc).
Sites are mostly dirt, staff are unhelpful.
It's close by the Forster beach, very nice view and can hear the sound of waves in the night times... Favourite for those who wants to stay long in the beach and kayak, SUP for fun
Absolutely amazing, had Karen at front office who took very good care of me and my family and has a beautiful beach close by.

Awesome location in the heart of Forster.  2 mins walk to beach & restaurants.  Been many times prior but never at Xmas.   The new managers are lovely.   Super friendly & made checking in on Boxing day a breeze by having a check in desk with info pack as you drove in.  Nothing was to much trouble they are a great asset to the park.    A few activities for kids & they had an afternoon of live music which was great.  New amenities & camp kitchen was really nice.   Our caravan site was large & really level.   The park has location location location 👍

Very disappointing stay, no grass on our “grass” site with the exception of the 20cm tall grass under our van and random clumps of weeds throughout the dirt, site hadn’t been cleaned prior to arrival, rubbish and random rusted bits of metal throughout. Roads are terrible, pot holes throughout, nothing to do for young children I.e no playground, pool, water play etc etc. Won’t be coming back again

Great affordable little cabin , everything you need for a relaxing seaside stay close to beach & shops all in walking distance with lots to explore by short drives aswel . I will be back as Forster has the best Ocean pool to swim , I highly recommend to stay @ Reflections Forster !

We just stayed for one night but it's definitely worth staying more. The location is interesting. The camp site has everything you need and more. Very polite, clean and quiet. You can walk to a few restaurants in just a few minutes. We were told you can see dolphins there sometimes.

I just want to say its amazing that you guys helped out “Inside a mans mind” Justin Cumner and Norman the forman.

Havent stayed here but the fact you guys were willing to help is awesome.

Great work guys.

We've stayed here before, the location can't be faulted for walking to restaurants etc, great for those that just want to stroll out for breakfast. We're in a van so no review's on the cabins but they do look very well set-up. Xx

We arrived to find that our booking had been canceled without our knowledge, and the substantial deposit was not returned. They offered another cabin, but for 3 adults instead of 5. We thought they would give a total refund but they wanted to charge us more even though two of us had to find accommodation elsewhere in the town!

We booked a deluxe cabin for 4 nights in October 2025.||Pros:|- Location of the park, it's 250m walk to the beach, with local restaurants and cafes surrounding. |- Mid-week booking discount|- Clean and tidy|- Nespresso machine and pods included with cabin stay||Cons: |- No on-site facilities for families to enjoy (no pool, playground or games room etc). There are board games available to use but that's it. |- No internal laundry. Communal laundry is available at $7 a load for washing (excluding laundry products) and $8 a load for drying.|- Kitchenette makes meals tricky. However there is a communal BBQ if required.|- The WiFi connection is non-existent! We were given password to connect but the connection is extremely poor and kept dropping out. We had to hotspot to be able to use the TV for streaming services.||As someone with school aged kids, we have no plans to use Reflections Forster again. This park may be a better fit for adult friends or retirees travelling together.

Very disappointed in park They had 3 attempts to find a site that was suitable for our van park grounds are in extremely poor condition and the internal roads of the park are a disgrace and extremely dangerous to walk on wouldnt recommend or be returning a massive let down
Such a breath of fresh air when I needed a break. Thankyou staff were lovley.

Park has seen some upgrades to the bathroom facilities and are very nice. Really needs a pool, heated as per Hawks Nest. Cannot comment on staff as its all contact free, checking in online and getting access codes via email. Is close to everything Forster has to offer.

Our stay from 21 -27 September on a powered site was our third time at Forster Beach Holiday Park, Every time we holiday there its gets better each time. Facilities are first class staff are courteous and friendly it is just a great park. We checked out the Parks at Tuncurry but Forster Beach is the pick for us.
Love it we will be back.
Regards John & Narelle McGregor
Awesome

For the price of the premium cabins I was definitely expecting a little more luxury $650 for 2 nights and only had got the basics. Some improvements I’d like to mention would be:
* softer bed possibly larger as well very small double.
*condiments cupboard just basic one eg. oil etc to cook with as it did state fully equipped kitchen
* cleaning the exhaust fan as this had heaps of fluff and mould on it.
*also checking bathroom for ants as we had a bath then had 10 ants appear in the bathroom
*heater/Aircon in the bedroom.

The location of the park is great I enjoyed that aspect and the large carpark as we had a boat on and were able to park next to our cabin for I was disappointed in the quality of the room for the price I was paying. If your going to charge that amount the room would want to be upgraded a bit

They really sorted us out. We had a great stay here. Pricing was reasonable, location was amazing. The places had everything they needed. Awesome stay.
Don't get put off by the HQ people that answer the phones. The people at reception and that helped with our room were all amazing!

Too many bad experiences to list. From check in, we had booked a month ago  - - - -  to Ants ,  cockroaches  and an overly crowded  non useable  lounge and dinning area . Reception was not very welcoming. Heads up

PROFITS OVER GUEST SAFETY AND ENJOYMENT!  The night we stayed, torrential rain hit. We live in the area and were only staying to come see some friends for the night. With all the flooding that’s been occurring in our area, we made contact earlier in the day and asked if we could postpone our booking to another time. We were rudely told we knew the conditions when we booked so NO!  The weather was so bad that not even an hour later the office was SMSing guests to warn them of the bad weather. We were a one night booking, it’s not like we were cancelling a two week holiday. We do this once or twice a year only to come see friends who travel to the area. Never again, we will not be back. I’m embarrassed at the lack of compassion shown from a local business in our beautiful area and that they care more about profits than they do their guests - Shame on them!!

Word's cannot describe reflections caravan Park
The staff where so friendly and helpful
Cabin we stayed in so clean and tidy
The van park was also  clean  and so close to all amenities including the shops
We loved so much we rebooked  it again
Once again a BIG thank you for a wonderful weekend away
See you next year
Great place for holidays. Close to shops cafes and beach.

My 5 stars to the guys who are managing this place, we had a very wonderful time staying. Amenities are top class and this was our best camp sites ever stayed. Kitchen is great here, class bathroom and very well maintained. Although we had to leave due to the heavy wind as our tents were almost in a flying situation, our stay was fantastic and we enjoyed every bit of it. Close to the shops, woollies is just a 4 min drive and this is located at a very central spot. This place is more secure than I thought. Keep up the good work.
Great location,  beautiful amenities, and friendly staff!!
Great location, shame there is no pool but all other facilities are fantastic including washing machines and dryer that send you a message when they are all done. Very happy with our stay

This park is in a great location close to the shops, restaurants, good walks and the beach but I wouldn’t recommend a premium waterfront site. Granted these sites have big double slabs which are great but they are also directly under huge pine trees and while they are beautiful to look at they aren’t so great being parked under as they (assisted by the cockatoos) drop pine nuts on your car and caravan continuously which sounds like hail and makes a terrible mess. The sites under the particularly large pines are also very dark. The premium sites also back onto the only road leading to the boat ramp and public carpark which appears to double as a meeting place for rev-heads, youths on electric bikes and scooters and hoons,  all of which use the road as a place to wheel spin and generally loudly show off especially at weekends. The sunsets over the water are beautiful but marred somewhat by the huge grey metal fence surrounding the park. The issues I raise are not the fault of the park. I’m just giving my opinion on the premium sites in particular as I was very disappointed by the one we were on.
Fantastic place to stay close to the water. Eco cabin was great, staff went above and beyond.

We stayed in one of the large static homes and the attention to detail was amazing.  It was clean, bright, in a lovely location, just a short walk to everything.
Would 100% recommend.

Unfortunately they have spoiled the park with the placement of cabins on all of the lower sites which now completely blocks any outlook you used to have of the river and gorgeous sunsets from caravan sites.  Completely changed the ambience of the park.  Still the location is great,  but it has gone down in our list of our "go to" parks.

Great location but that is where it ends. No children facilities except for a dump sand pile on the way in with a few sand buckets.
Beautiful new camp kitchen but no power points that can be used. The new amenities block is awesome but it’s always dirty and never cleaned.
Our site was like a mud pit. Every time it rained it was under 2 inches of water and the water would not drain away.
We ended up leaving 3 days early as the mud being walked through our caravan was horrible.
Trying to get in contact with reception is useless. Firstly told that the girl that looks after bookings is off sick. Called back the next day and she is now out. Still awaiting  call back. Tried to call this morning and the phone just rings out after around 1 minute then goes to voicemail mail.

We have stayed many times at different reflections caravan parks. This one has to be the worse one we have stayed at.

Thankyou to Lat and the house keeping team, very professional service and makes us feel at home every time.
Thank you to Ray as well for always being so accomodating. Our family stays here every Easter and we look forward to visiting our home away from home each time.
Fantastic staff, location and place. Definitely worth a visit.
Lovely spot, new toilets and showers are nice.
Well maintained spot. Walking distance to beach, shops and dolphin watching.
Great place to chill close to the main shopping street and beach

Great booking system, very friendly and helpful staff, beautifully maintained, new and modern facilities, perfect location minutes from beach, marina, shops in main street, surf club, great options for accommodation and very quiet. Highly recommended.

Great facilities and location.
Very tight packed sites though.  Had a new bike stolen overnight while locked to wheel of motorhome. And outboard was also stolen I believe from someone else.
The amount Reflections charges for everything it should be able to afford much better security.  I won't be staying there again until it's more secure.

we stayed in a deluxe cabin and our room was infested with ants and cockroaches, was super gross and scary. i hate cockroaches and we were there for 3 nights, saw a big roach each night and ants were in the kitchen, bathroom and bedrooms.
but overall the location is great and decent space for 5 people

Nice park in a great location. Original site allocated didn't suit our Motorhome as we could level the vehicle on the slope. Staff happily offer us alternate sites to choose from. The park is a little dated and pokey but it looks like they are making improvement. New amenities block was clean however already showing signs of wear and tear. Price is up there but would happily stay again.
Great caravan park
Took our camper for the week. Great spot. Close to everything. Amenities are clean as a whistle. Friendly helpful staff.

Forster never disappoints. Reflections has a fabulous location with most things within walking distance. Could not fault our stay at the holiday park in the fully accessible air conditioned cabin. Beautifully set out with enough room to park our car and boat. Spacious inside enabling our daughter to use her wheelchair. Bathroom fully accessible. Kitchen fully equipped. Justine is a great host and all the staff are very helpful and obliging. Thank you for a very enjoyable holiday.

This caravan park has the best location in Forster, nestled between the Marina, Beach and shops. Amenities are great and support staff are helpful. Unfortunately like many caravan sites it appears to be favoring cabin, which is likely a cash grab.
BAD SERVICE. After our booking they informed us that the campsite was just for a tent and not our campervan. NEVER GOT OUR MONEY BACK.
Nice caravan park close to the water.
Great park.

We stayed on a powered campsite for 1 night, the prices do not equal what you get (62 dollars for 1night). The showers where outdated, not fresh + weak shower hose. It's nice that it's so central if the town was worth while but sadly Forster felt like an ugly beach town on the Belgium coast 😭, nah its not that bad but definitely seen some more pritty beach towns like Coffs Harbour and so would recommend to spend your money elsewhere!
On a good note the staff where friendly 👍
A great park to stay at! Very close to town, great facilities and great staff.

G8 park, g8 place, g8 staff, g8 location. On 5th Sept 2024 we spent 1 night at the park on a powered site. I was quite shocked when I was charged $63 for a night's accommodation, I should've done my homework and rang around before I paid by credit card   because after contacting NRMA Tuncurry my accommodation wouldve been $45 per night including my  NRMA discount. We are members of Reflections rewards club and are loyal visitors to the caravan park since 1993  we thought the price $63 per night was extremely expensive and we will never use Forster Beach caravan park for an overnight stay as loyal users of the park we thought we were completely ripped off  now we know why the park was empty.

Great spot. NO Wifi for the TV if you want to watch a family movie. Only for your phone. After-hours contact is they leave an email message for the next day to address an issue. We had no gas in our BBQ had to wait the next day to use it. Will stay somewhere else next time. At $400 a night we should have had gas and access to wifi.
To expansive for 1 night
Great location friendly staff very clean premises

They don't know the meaning of the words customer service. Found them to be completely unreasonable and unprofessional in dealing with management regarding a refund. I will never stay at a reflections park again!
A great CP with good space a great location and lovely helpful service. Handy to beach, cafes, lookout and shops.

I can't ask for more, excellent views, very close to town and the marina. The cabins are spacious and well appointed. A beer on the verandah watching the sun set over the river, great way to end the day.
I stayed in a cabin. It's ok if you're there alone. The bathrooms are tiny. If you had 4 people it would be really cramped

A lovely park close to everything.  Spent 2 nights in one of the cabins.  The cabin had everything we needed for a lovely stay including a kitchen with ample equipment,  comfortable beds a 2 seater couch and a lovely outdoor seating area. The shower was lovely and hot but quite cramped, would not be comfortable for a large sized person.  It is possible to walk everywhere including into Tuncurry

Well maintained park, sites are generous with water, power and sullage. Great outlook from sites along marina. Close to shops and eateries. Sites over the back can be difficult to access if you have a low van. Amenities are well maintained.
Close to pubs &restaurants
Two of us stayed at Reflections Forster Beach Holiday Park whilst on a coastal road trip. Friendly staff, access to good ammenities, and well-maintained park. Thank you for our stay!

Have stayed at the park for many years, but getting disappointed by how run down the park is becoming. All the roads are in a bad state of disrepair and are very dangerous especially for elderly people walking around as there are large pot holes and loose gravel everywhere. The park is now looking very outdated compared to other parks in the area. The bins are only emptied a few times a week and the general waste bins were full of maggots. For the substantial increase in fees each year, you would expect a little more from the park.
Loved staying here, was only for one night but can’t wait to go back and spend more time in area. Friendly staff, very accommodating for our big van at short notice. Well kept park!

Loved the park. Will come back for sure. Marked the rooms down to 4 stars partly because we brought our own (caravan) but because the roads are more like laneways and are difficult to enter and exit at either end.  Spots are generous with three car widths each (one for can, a second for awning and a third to park car).

Service at front desk was lovely and helpful.  Park put on music and various other holiday time activities.

Location is excellent being right on the lake and beach.
Tight spots. Close to shops and beach.  Great location

Wir haben versehentlich im Reflections Bonny Hills und im Reflections Forster gebucht. Direkt nach dem administrativen Fehler haben wir angerufen, um unseren Fehler zu melden und nur eine Buchung zu stornieren. Dies wurde telefonisch bestätigt. Allerdings wurde die doppelte Buchung nie storniert und nachträglich erhielten wir lediglich einen Gutschein für den Preis (1 Jahr gültig)! Wir leben in der Schweiz und können keinen Gutschein gebrauchen... Nach mehrfachem Nachfragen habe ich lediglich die "Rules & Conditions" erhalten und schliesslich gar keine Antwort. Extrem schlechter Kundenservice und 0.0 Flexibilität beim Service.

Very disappointing. Paid extra for boat space and every time we went to park in our spot we paid for someone was parked in it. Tried to get staff to help but they just made it more difficult. Rooms are outdated over pricedand dirty
The actual powered site we were given was a real disappointment, uneven, unlevel, and overpriced for what we were given. Amenities very good as well as the location next to water.
Exc

Could have been cleaned better
Oil leak of some sort on bedside table
Smears on fridge and microwave
Noticed mould appearing in corners of rooms
Nice & relaxed

Always a great place to stay. Close to everything u need.
Staff are always willing to help

Very disappointing
Two couples staying in a cabin,  plenty of parking space yet only one car allowed in park.
Cabin is old and run down,  decking feels like it is going to collapse under you.
All kitchen utensils had to be washed as everything was filthy especially the glasses,  looked like a 2 year old had washed them.
Only thing going for the park is the closeness to the shops etc.
The big 4 in tuncurry is a much better place to stay,  only staying here as friends are here

I was told I couldn't have the site I wanted because the toilet block was closed and the council regulations had to be within so far from campsites. Why not just open the toilet block. Was will open it for school holidays. What was the big deal. I always thought look after the customer. Also every time we go there there is noise. Last time demolition was being performed and we had a big jack hammer all day going for our 2 day stay and this time was told after we set up camp and paid road works were being performed at the entrance round about for two nights. So we had no sleep for two nights. Why weren't we told on check in and offered a reduction in price. No they waited till 5pm by text to inform us.  I have been going there for years but  now every time you go there now there is a new rule change or there is noise all day or all night. I am seriously considering switching parks I Forster

As mentioned below, this place is overpriced for what you get. Nothing fun for the kids to do, especially in winter when you don't really go to the beach. No views of anything nice. The male park manager was rude, obnoxious and on his high horse.

Our camp site was so small, our camper trailer only just fit but we couldn't set the whole thing up. Our car had to almost be parked on the road.

Overnight we heard a massive bang, but thought it was a garbage truck. Come to find out my car had been smashed into by the park managers car, with a note left on my windscreen. At first I thought, accidents happen. Unfortunately there was no sorry, ever.. no sorry on the note, no sorry from the mangers, no sorry from the ladies in the office, no sorry from head office.
The first sorry I got was from MY insurance company. What a pain in the *** my last 3 months have been, since my stay in June.

I wouldn't recommend this park to anyone, do yourself a favour and try somewhere else in Forster!

And no need for staff to reply to my message now with any 'sorrys'.. too little too late.

I rented a cabin for the weekend with my wife. When we arrived, the receptionist greeted us warmly and directed us to our cabin. At first glance, the cabin was outdated and in need of repair, but because we were not booking a stay in a five-star hotel, we dismissed that problem. Instead, we left our luggage in the cabin and went out to supper. After returning from Dinner and a walk, I needed to warm up, so I turned on the water to start a shower. After soaping myself, the water turned ice cold, so I tried to turn it off, but the tap kept spinning and spinning until the handle fell off in my Hand.
angry, cold, and soap covered, I used a towel to dry off and realised that the only heater I had was a strip heater.   When we finally decided to go to bed, I pulled back the covers to discover that they had not been changed since they were filthy with dust and hair.  I requested a callback by sending an SMS to the specified number. Nobody did.
The next morning, I went into the office and started to tell my story to the receptionist, when the Manager Eddy holding a child mentioned he saw the SMS and asked why I did not call him???   I said that I was unsure if someone was awake and decided to do the right thing and SMS and wait for a response. After telling him what had happened, he said, "What do you want me to do about it?  it wasn't if we were being Malicious" I calmly and quickly explained that I understood that, yet I needed to resolve the issue. I asked him if he could move us to a cabin that worked and was clean, he said "there is none available."  Angry yet trying to keep my head, I walked out the door then turned around and said “I know what you can do you can credit me and I will find another accommodation place to stay he agreed.   It has been 8 Days now and still no credit.
My Belief is that the Manager Eddy Should Not be there he has Zero People Skills.
Not kids friendly park it is for the elderly will never take family back there management Justine was quite rude definitely not a family holiday spot won’t be going back
Definitely not a family oriented or kid friendly holiday park !!!

Awful. Uneven sites covered in cigarette butts, portable shower block main amenities closed up. Traffic noise terrible . Warnings on paperwork to lock everything up and secure bikes,kayaks etc ....certainly didn't have a good feel ...
Great view over water .

Great park , great location stayed in both top cabins on hill. 2nd cabin has a beautiful view, but can't see it as the hedge is grown up, Really need clipping down lower. A waste of a good view. If not clipped will have to consider going elsewhere.

Everything done via constant annoying txt messages
Didn't see one staff member all weekend .. cut tree with leaves and a branch poking out scratched the whole side of our brand new caravan ...limb was hidden by leaves ..sites are very cramped up the far end

What a great spot! Right in the middle of everything.
Staff were wonderful, amenities were tidy
Great location facilities were clean and well maintained

Dragging the old van up to Forster was worth it.
Lovely clean, well maintained sites, friendly staff, walking distance to town and fishing.
